ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 covers toast as a food product; grilled sliced bread is a close variant. No exact code for Chinese-style grilled sliced bread exists in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers bread and similar products as the nearest defensible parent category. No specific code for grilled sliced bread exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559003 covers toast as a substance; used as closest parent for grilled sliced bread.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is three slices of grilled bread, approximately 90 g total, as served at Wan Jia Fu Chinese Restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Bread, white, commercially prepared, toasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18069)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for white bread, commercially prepared, toasted (NDB 18069 / FDC ID 168012). Grilling produces a similar moisture-loss and Maillard browning effect to toasting; nutrient profile is considered equivalent for database purposes. No Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry for plain grilled sliced bread was identified.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — white bread, toasted
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 70 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for white bread (toasted). Grilled sliced bread made from standard white sandwich bread is nutritionally equivalent to toasted white bread for GI purposes. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 52.0) / 100 = 36.4, where carbohydrate per 90 g serving = 57.8 × 0.9 = 52.0 g.

hypertension.guidance: A 90 g serving of grilled sliced bread contributes approximately 442 mg sodium, representing about 19% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; portion control and avoidance of high-sodium toppings such as salted butter or soy sauce are advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index and high glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 70 and a glycemic load of approximately 36 per 90 g serving, grilled white sliced bread causes a rapid postprandial blood glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, pair with protein or healthy fat to blunt the glycemic response, or substitute with lower-GI wholegrain bread.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of refined white bread is associated with elevated triglycerides and modest cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should prefer wholegrain alternatives and monitor total sodium intake from bread and toppings combined.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Sliced Bread (烤面包片) is a simple bread dish served in Chinese restaurants in Singapore, consisting of standard white sandwich bread slices that are grilled or toasted until the surface develops a golden-brown crust through Maillard browning. The texture is crisp on the outside and soft on the inside. It is typically served as an accompaniment to roasted meats, soups, or congee, or as a standalone starter. In the context of Wan Jia Fu Chinese Restaurant in Singapore, it is served as three pieces (3片) per order. Nutritionally, grilled white sliced bread is a high-carbohydrate, moderate-protein food with a high glycemic index (GI 70), making it a significant source of rapidly digestible starch. Each 90 g serving provides approximately 282 kcal, 52 g carbohydrate, 9.6 g protein, and 3.8 g fat, along with 442 mg sodium. The bread is enriched with B vitamins including thiamine, riboflavin, niacin, and folate. It contains gluten and wheat allergens. Clinically, the high GI and glycemic load are relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes and metabolic syndrome. The moderate sodium content is relevant for hypertensive patients. It is vegetarian, vegan, and halal-suitable when prepared without animal-derived toppings.
common_user_queries: What is grilled sliced bread in Chinese restaurants?
How many calories in 3 slices of grilled bread?
Is grilled bread suitable for diabetics?
Glycemic index of toasted white bread

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry for grilled sliced bread as served in a Chinese restaurant context was identified. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for white bread, commercially prepared, toasted (FDC ID 168012), which is the closest approved-source match. GI is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for white toasted bread. Confidence is medium due to source adaptation rather than exact match.
notes: Serving size of 90 g is estimated from the menu description of three pieces (3片); a standard sandwich bread slice weighs approximately 28–32 g, giving a three-slice serving of approximately 84–96 g; 90 g is used as the midpoint estimate. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for commercially prepared white bread. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for plain white bread with no animal-derived ingredients. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; white bread contains no vitamin C. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for standard white bread. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; white bread is not a source of B12 unless fortified. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; white bread is not a significant source of vitamin D. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all reported from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 168012. GI source is the University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate based on white toasted bread. Cuisine tags from input (Chinese, Roasted Duck) are preserved as context; the food itself is a plain bread accompaniment. FoodEx2 code A000E is used as closest parent; no specific FoodEx2 code for grilled sliced bread exists. SNOMED CT 226559003 is used as closest parent for toast as a substance.
